ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Screwdriver cocktail; closest parent is alcoholic beverage food product (FOODON_03301463).
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No confirmed valid FoodEx2 code for spirit-based mixed cocktails (Screwdriver) could be verified against the official EFSA FoodEx2 catalogue. Setting to null per ontology honesty policy rather than risk an unverifiable code. FoodOn closest_parent anchor (FOODON_03301463) provides the primary ontology coverage for this record.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 53041004 (Alcoholic beverage) is the closest defensible parent; no specific cocktail code exists in SNOMED CT.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Screwdriver cocktail served in a highball or rocks glass, typically containing approximately 45 ml vodka topped with 195 ml fresh or commercial orange juice over ice, totalling approximately 240 g.

nutrient_source.title: Alcoholic beverage, cocktail, screwdriver — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB No. 14053)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry 'Alcoholic beverage, cocktail, screwdriver' (FDC ID 174831 / NDB 14053) provides per-100 g nutrient values for the standard vodka-and-orange-juice cocktail. Values used directly.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal) / University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the Screwdriver cocktail. GI is estimated from its dominant glycemic component: orange juice. The University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008, Diabetes Care) report orange juice GI at approximately 50–57 (mean ~52). Vodka contributes alcohol calories but negligible glycemic carbohydrate. A blended GI of 52 is applied, reflecting the orange juice fraction as the primary glycemic driver. gi_category is 'low' per the ≤55 threshold rule. glycemic_load_per_serving = (52 × 13.92 g carbohydrate per 240 g serving) / 100 = 7.24, rounded to 7.2.

hypertension.key_factor: Alcohol content and moderate caloric density
hypertension.guidance: Regular alcohol consumption raises blood pressure; individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid alcoholic cocktails such as the Screwdriver, as even moderate intake (>1 standard drink/day) is associated with dose-dependent increases in systolic blood pressure per WHO and AHA guidelines.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Alcohol-carbohydrate interaction and postprandial glucose variability
type2_diabetes.guidance: The Screwdriver contains approximately 5.8 g carbohydrate per 100 g (primarily from orange juice sugars), and alcohol can mask hypoglycaemia symptoms; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this cocktail only with food, monitor blood glucose carefully, and account for the glycemic load (~7 per serving) within their daily carbohydrate budget.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Alcohol-mediated cardiovascular risk and caloric contribution
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While moderate orange juice consumption provides vitamin C and potassium with potential cardioprotective effects, the alcohol component of the Screwdriver is associated with increased triglycerides, arrhythmia risk, and cardiomyopathy with habitual use; individuals with cardiovascular disease should follow their clinician's guidance on alcohol limits, typically no more than one standard drink per day.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Screwdriver is a classic two-ingredient alcoholic cocktail made by combining vodka and orange juice, typically served over ice in a highball or rocks glass. The standard ratio is approximately one part vodka (40% ABV) to three or four parts fresh or commercial orange juice. The drink is light orange in colour, mildly sweet, and citrusy, with the sharpness of vodka softened by the natural sugars and acidity of the orange juice. It is one of the most widely recognised cocktails internationally and appears on bar menus across the United States, Europe, Southeast Asia, and Latin America. In Singapore, it is available at Western-style bars, hotel lounges, and Mexican-themed restaurants such as Fiesta Mexico. Nutritionally, a 240 g serving provides approximately 120 kcal, 13.9 g carbohydrate (primarily from orange juice sugars), 0.7 g protein, and 0.2 g fat. The orange juice fraction contributes vitamin C (~32 mg per serving), potassium (~182 mg), and folate. The glycemic index is estimated at 52 (low category), with a glycemic load of approximately 7 per serving. Clinically, the alcohol content warrants caution for individuals with hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and cardiovascular disease. The cocktail is gluten-free and dairy-free but is not halal or kosher due to its alcohol content.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a Screwdriver?
What is in a Screwdriver drink?

confidence_reason: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy contains an exact entry for 'Alcoholic beverage, cocktail, screwdriver' (FDC ID 174831), providing per-100 g nutrient values directly applicable to this record. GI is estimated from the orange juice component using the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008), which is the standard analogous-estimate approach for cocktails with no direct GI measurement. FoodOn and SNOMED CT ontology anchors are real and defensible closest-parent codes; FoodEx2 is set to null with a documented gap note per ontology honesty policy.
notes: 1. The input food_name 'Screw driver' has been normalised to the canonical English name 'Screwdriver Cocktail'. The two-word variant 'Screw driver' is retained as an alias. 2. The cuisine tag 'Mexican' in the input reflects the restaurant context (Fiesta Mexico) rather than the cocktail's origin; the cocktail itself is of American/international origin and the cuisine field has been corrected accordingly. 3. saturated_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 14053); naturally zero as this is a spirit-and-juice beverage with negligible fat. monounsaturated_fat_g is 0.0 and polyunsaturated_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA source. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — naturally zero for this beverage. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — naturally zero as this beverage contains no animal-derived fat. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA source; naturally zero as this is a plant-derived and spirit beverage. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA source; naturally zero for this beverage. 4. GI is an analogous estimate based on orange juice GI (~52) from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008, Diabetes Care); no direct GI measurement for the Screwdriver cocktail exists in the approved literature. 5. Multilingual local names are not applicable for this Western/international cocktail; the G6 multilingual rule applies to Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, and Vietnamese dishes — this record does not fall into any of those mandatory categories. 6. foodex2_code is null — no confirmed valid FoodEx2 code for spirit-based mixed cocktails could be verified against the official EFSA FoodEx2 catalogue; set to null per ontology honesty policy with match_type 'none'. FoodOn (FOODON_03301463) and SNOMED CT (53041004) provide sufficient ontology coverage. 7. All remaining -1 values in per_100g represent nutrients not separately reported in the USDA SR Legacy entry for this cocktail item; these are not confirmed zeros but rather unreported values in the source.

Iteration 2: Verified aliases_by_language consistency with flat aliases list. Confirmed all zero values documented with justification phrases in notes. Confirmed all -1 values documented with source-gap explanation. Confirmed GI threshold: GI=52 ≤ 55 → gi_category='low'. Confirmed ontology match_type values are only 'exact', 'closest_parent', or 'none'.

Replaced with null and match_type 'none' per ontology honesty policy — no confirmed valid FoodEx2 code could be verified against the official EFSA FoodEx2 catalogue. FoodOn (FOODON_03301463) and SNOMED CT (53041004) provide sufficient defensible ontology coverage. Gap note added to foodex2_gap_note and notes fields. All other sections preserved from iteration 2.
